mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, invalidating the recession fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where demand is shifting from goods to services, reducing corporate earnings visibility and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across three consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, rising core inflation readings force the market to price in prolonged restrictive policy, which increases the cost of capital and suppresses S&P 500 valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when both FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month, contradicting the discount rate channel.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in housing starts reflects underlying consumer confidence and construction sector momentum, which historically leads positive S&P 500 equity returns over a 5-day trading window as market participants price in near-term economic resilience.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 12-month rolling window, indicating the housing-to-equity transmission channel has broken down.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%, the labor market exhibits overheating dynamics that historically precede aggressive monetary tightening, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces a positive Sharpe ratio over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while real consumer credit contracts signals a shift toward savings and deleveraging, which historically reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with the wrong sign or fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth, a simultaneous rise in the federal funds rate creates a liquidity drag that outweighs fundamental earnings support, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples compress.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% and the Fed funds rate rises, disproving the liquidity drag hypothesis.
